exercises.solution:
import numpy as np

# Pauli matrices
I = np.eye(2, dtype=complex)
X = np.array([[0, 1], [1, 0]], dtype=complex)
Y = np.array([[0, -1j], [1j, 0]], dtype=complex)
Z = np.array([[1, 0], [0, -1]], dtype=complex)

def depolarizing_channel(rho, p):
    """Apply depolarizing channel: (1-p)rho + p*I/2"""
    return (1 - p) * rho + p * I / 2

def amplitude_damping_channel(rho, gamma):
    """Apply amplitude damping channel."""
    K0 = np.array([[1, 0], [0, np.sqrt(1 - gamma)]], dtype=complex)
    K1 = np.array([[0, np.sqrt(gamma)], [0, 0]], dtype=complex)
    return K0 @ rho @ K0.conj().T + K1 @ rho @ K1.conj().T

def phase_damping_channel(rho, lambda_):
    """Apply phase damping channel."""
    K0 = np.array([[1, 0], [0, np.sqrt(1 - lambda_)]], dtype=complex)
    K1 = np.array([[0, 0], [0, np.sqrt(lambda_)]], dtype=complex)
    return K0 @ rho @ K0.conj().T + K1 @ rho @ K1.conj().T

def is_unital(channel_func, param, tol=1e-10):
    """Check if channel preserves maximally mixed state."""
    rho_max = I / 2
    output = channel_func(rho_max, param)
    return np.allclose(output, rho_max, atol=tol)

def bloch_vector(rho):
    """Extract Bloch vector from density matrix."""
    rx = np.real(np.trace(rho @ X))
    ry = np.real(np.trace(rho @ Y))
    rz = np.real(np.trace(rho @ Z))
    return np.array([rx, ry, rz])

# Test all channels
print("Common Quantum Channels Analysis")
print("=" * 50)

# Parameters
p = 0.2
gamma = 0.3
lambda_ = 0.4

# Test unitality
print("\nUnitality Check:")
print(f"  Depolarizing: {is_unital(depolarizing_channel, p)}")
print(f"  Amplitude Damping: {is_unital(amplitude_damping_channel, gamma)}")
print(f"  Phase Damping: {is_unital(phase_damping_channel, lambda_)}")

# Test on |+> state
rho_plus = np.array([[0.5, 0.5], [0.5, 0.5]], dtype=complex)

print("\nEffect on |+> state:")
print(f"  Input Bloch: {bloch_vector(rho_plus).round(3)}")

for name, channel, param in [
    ("Depolarizing", depolarizing_channel, p),
    ("Amplitude Damping", amplitude_damping_channel, gamma),
    ("Phase Damping", phase_damping_channel, lambda_)
]:
    output = channel(rho_plus, param)
    r_out = bloch_vector(output)
    print(f"  {name}: {r_out.round(3)}")

# Verify trace preservation
print("\nTrace preservation:")
rho_test = np.array([[0.6, 0.3], [0.3, 0.4]], dtype=complex)
for name, channel, param in [
    ("Depolarizing", depolarizing_channel, p),
    ("Amplitude Damping", amplitude_damping_channel, gamma),
    ("Phase Damping", phase_damping_channel, lambda_)
]:
    output = channel(rho_test, param)
    tr = np.trace(output)
    print(f"  {name}: Tr = {tr.real:.6f}")
hints: Amplitude damping K0 = diag(1, sqrt(1-gamma)), K1 has sqrt(gamma) in [0,1] position
A channel is unital if E(I/d) = I/d

exercises.title: T1/T2 Relaxation Simulator
exercises.description: Build a simulator that models realistic T1/T2 relaxation for superconducting qubits. Simulate the decay of excited states and coherences over time.

    """
    Fit exponential decay to extract T1 and T2.
    """
    # Your code here

# Simulate superconducting qubit
T1 = 50e-6  # 50 us
T2 = 30e-6  # 30 us
t_total = 200e-6  # 200 us
n_steps = 100

# Start in |1> for T1 measurement
rho_1 = np.array([[0, 0], [0, 1]], dtype=complex)

exercises.solution:
import numpy as np

def amplitude_damping_kraus(gamma):
    K0 = np.array([[1, 0], [0, np.sqrt(1-gamma)]], dtype=complex)
    K1 = np.array([[0, np.sqrt(gamma)], [0, 0]], dtype=complex)
    return [K0, K1]

def phase_damping_kraus(lambda_):
    K0 = np.array([[1, 0], [0, np.sqrt(1-lambda_)]], dtype=complex)
    K1 = np.array([[0, 0], [0, np.sqrt(lambda_)]], dtype=complex)
    return [K0, K1]

def apply_channel(rho, kraus_ops):
    return sum(K @ rho @ K.conj().T for K in kraus_ops)

def combined_relaxation_kraus(t, T1, T2):
    """
    Create Kraus operators for combined T1 and T2 relaxation.
    """
    # Amplitude damping parameter
    gamma = 1 - np.exp(-t / T1)
    
    # Pure dephasing: 1/T_phi = 1/T2 - 1/(2*T1)
    if T2 < 2 * T1:
        T_phi = 1 / (1/T2 - 1/(2*T1))
        lambda_ = 1 - np.exp(-t / T_phi)
    else:
        lambda_ = 0  # No pure dephasing needed
    
    # Compose amplitude damping and phase damping
    kraus_ad = amplitude_damping_kraus(gamma)
    kraus_pd = phase_damping_kraus(lambda_)
    
    # Combined Kraus
    combined = [L @ K for L in kraus_pd for K in kraus_ad]
    return combined

def simulate_relaxation(rho_init, T1, T2, t_total, n_steps):
    """
    Simulate qubit relaxation.
    """
    dt = t_total / n_steps
    
    times = []
    populations = []  # P(|1>)
    coherences = []   # |rho_01|
    
    rho = rho_init.copy()
    
    for i in range(n_steps + 1):
        t = i * dt
        times.append(t)
        populations.append(np.real(rho[1, 1]))
        coherences.append(np.abs(rho[0, 1]))
        
        if i < n_steps:
            kraus = combined_relaxation_kraus(dt, T1, T2)
            rho = apply_channel(rho, kraus)
    
    return np.array(times), np.array(populations), np.array(coherences)

def fit_decay_times(times, populations, coherences):
    """
    Fit exponential decay to extract T1 and T2.
    Uses linear regression on log data.
    """
    # T1 from population decay: P(t) = P(0) * exp(-t/T1)
    valid_pop = populations > 1e-10
    if np.sum(valid_pop) > 2:
        log_pop = np.log(populations[valid_pop])
        t_pop = times[valid_pop]
        # Linear fit: log(P) = log(P0) - t/T1
        coeffs_pop = np.polyfit(t_pop, log_pop, 1)
        T1_fit = -1 / coeffs_pop[0]
    else:
        T1_fit = np.inf
    
    # T2 from coherence decay: |rho_01|(t) = |rho_01|(0) * exp(-t/T2)
    valid_coh = coherences > 1e-10
    if np.sum(valid_coh) > 2:
        log_coh = np.log(coherences[valid_coh])
        t_coh = times[valid_coh]
        coeffs_coh = np.polyfit(t_coh, log_coh, 1)
        T2_fit = -1 / coeffs_coh[0]
    else:
        T2_fit = np.inf
    
    return T1_fit, T2_fit

# Simulate superconducting qubit
print("T1/T2 Relaxation Simulation")
print("=" * 50)

T1 = 50e-6  # 50 us
T2 = 30e-6  # 30 us
t_total = 200e-6  # 200 us
n_steps = 200

# T1 measurement: start in |1>
rho_1 = np.array([[0, 0], [0, 1]], dtype=complex)
times, pops, _ = simulate_relaxation(rho_1, T1, T2, t_total, n_steps)
T1_fit, _ = fit_decay_times(times, pops, np.ones_like(pops))

print(f"\nT1 Measurement (starting from |1>):")
print(f"  Actual T1: {T1*1e6:.1f} us")
print(f"  Fitted T1: {T1_fit*1e6:.1f} us")

# T2 measurement: start in |+>
rho_plus = np.array([[0.5, 0.5], [0.5, 0.5]], dtype=complex)
times, pops, cohs = simulate_relaxation(rho_plus, T1, T2, t_total, n_steps)
_, T2_fit = fit_decay_times(times, np.ones_like(pops), cohs)

print(f"\nT2 Measurement (starting from |+>):")
print(f"  Actual T2: {T2*1e6:.1f} us")
print(f"  Fitted T2: {T2_fit*1e6:.1f} us")

# Print decay curve
print(f"\nDecay curve (|+> state):")
print(f"{'Time (us)':<12} {'Population':<12} {'Coherence':<12}")
print("-" * 36)
for i in range(0, n_steps + 1, n_steps // 5):
    print(f"{times[i]*1e6:<12.1f} {pops[i]:<12.4f} {cohs[i]:<12.4f}")
hints: Use gamma = 1 - exp(-t/T1) for amplitude damping
Compose amplitude damping and phase damping channels

exercises.title: Bloch Sphere Trajectory Analyzer
exercises.description: Analyze how different noise channels affect trajectories on the Bloch sphere. Compare the contraction behavior of each channel type.
exercises.starterCode:
import numpy as np

def bloch_trajectory(channel_func, param, n_steps):
    """
    Compute Bloch sphere trajectory under repeated channel application.
    
    Returns:
        List of Bloch vectors [(rx, ry, rz), ...]
    """
    # Your code here

def analyze_contraction(channel_func, param):
    """
    Analyze how channel contracts the Bloch sphere.
    
    Returns:
        Dictionary with contraction factors for x, y, z directions

    pass

def find_fixed_points(channel_func, param, n_random=100):
    """
    Find fixed points of the channel by testing random states.
    
    Returns:
        List of (approximately) fixed states
    """
    # Your code here
    pass

# Analyze all three channels
channels = {
    'Depolarizing': (depolarizing_channel, 0.2),
    'Amplitude Damping': (amplitude_damping_channel, 0.3),
    'Phase Damping': (phase_damping_channel, 0.4)
}

for name, (channel, param) in channels.items():
    print(f"\n{name}:")
    contraction = analyze_contraction(channel, param)
    print(f"  Contraction: {contraction}")
exercises.solution:
import numpy as np

# Pauli matrices
I = np.eye(2, dtype=complex)
X = np.array([[0, 1], [1, 0]], dtype=complex)
Y = np.array([[0, -1j], [1j, 0]], dtype=complex)
Z = np.array([[1, 0], [0, -1]], dtype=complex)

def depolarizing_channel(rho, p):
    return (1 - p) * rho + p * I / 2

def amplitude_damping_channel(rho, gamma):
    K0 = np.array([[1, 0], [0, np.sqrt(1 - gamma)]], dtype=complex)
    K1 = np.array([[0, np.sqrt(gamma)], [0, 0]], dtype=complex)
    return K0 @ rho @ K0.conj().T + K1 @ rho @ K1.conj().T

def phase_damping_channel(rho, lambda_):
    K0 = np.array([[1, 0], [0, np.sqrt(1 - lambda_)]], dtype=complex)
    K1 = np.array([[0, 0], [0, np.sqrt(lambda_)]], dtype=complex)
    return K0 @ rho @ K0.conj().T + K1 @ rho @ K1.conj().T

def bloch_vector(rho):
    rx = np.real(np.trace(rho @ X))
    ry = np.real(np.trace(rho @ Y))
    rz = np.real(np.trace(rho @ Z))
    return np.array([rx, ry, rz])

def rho_from_bloch(r):
    return (I + r[0]*X + r[1]*Y + r[2]*Z) / 2

def bloch_trajectory(channel_func, param, n_steps, r_init=None):
    """
    Compute Bloch sphere trajectory under repeated channel application.
    """
    if r_init is None:
        r_init = np.array([1, 0, 0])  # Start at |+>
    
    rho = rho_from_bloch(r_init)
    trajectory = [bloch_vector(rho)]
    
    for _ in range(n_steps):
        rho = channel_func(rho, param)
        trajectory.append(bloch_vector(rho))
    
    return np.array(trajectory)

def analyze_contraction(channel_func, param):
    """
    Analyze how channel contracts the Bloch sphere.
    """
    # Test states along each axis
    test_states = {
        'x': np.array([1, 0, 0]),
        'y': np.array([0, 1, 0]),
        'z': np.array([0, 0, 1]),
        '-z': np.array([0, 0, -1])
    }
    
    contractions = {}
    shifts = {}
    
    for axis, r_init in test_states.items():
        rho = rho_from_bloch(r_init)
        rho_out = channel_func(rho, param)
        r_out = bloch_vector(rho_out)
        
        # Contraction factor along this direction
        idx = {'x': 0, 'y': 1, 'z': 2, '-z': 2}[axis]
        if abs(r_init[idx]) > 1e-10:
            contractions[axis] = r_out[idx] / r_init[idx]
        else:
            contractions[axis] = 0
        
        # Shift (for amplitude damping)
        shifts[axis] = r_out - contractions[axis] * r_init
    
    return {'contractions': contractions, 'shifts': shifts}

def find_fixed_points(channel_func, param, n_random=100, tol=1e-6):
    """
    Find fixed points of the channel by testing random states.
    """
    fixed_points = []
    
    # Test random pure states
    for _ in range(n_random):
        # Random Bloch vector on surface
        theta = np.random.uniform(0, np.pi)
        phi = np.random.uniform(0, 2*np.pi)
        r = np.array([np.sin(theta)*np.cos(phi), 
                      np.sin(theta)*np.sin(phi),
                      np.cos(theta)])
        
        rho = rho_from_bloch(r)
        rho_out = channel_func(rho, param)
        
        if np.allclose(rho, rho_out, atol=tol):
            fixed_points.append(r)
    
    # Also test special states
    special = [
        np.array([0, 0, 1]),   # |0>
        np.array([0, 0, -1]),  # |1>
        np.array([0, 0, 0])    # maximally mixed
    ]
    
    for r in special:
        rho = rho_from_bloch(r)
        rho_out = channel_func(rho, param)
        if np.allclose(rho, rho_out, atol=tol):
            if not any(np.allclose(r, fp, atol=tol) for fp in fixed_points):
                fixed_points.append(r)
    
    return fixed_points

# Analyze all three channels
print("Bloch Sphere Trajectory Analysis")
print("=" * 60)

channels = {
    'Depolarizing': (depolarizing_channel, 0.2),
    'Amplitude Damping': (amplitude_damping_channel, 0.3),
    'Phase Damping': (phase_damping_channel, 0.4)
}

for name, (channel, param) in channels.items():
    print(f"\n{name} (param={param}):")
    print("-" * 40)
    
    # Contraction analysis
    analysis = analyze_contraction(channel, param)
    print("  Contraction factors:")
    for axis, factor in analysis['contractions'].items():
        print(f"    {axis}: {factor:.4f}")
    
    # Fixed points
    fixed = find_fixed_points(channel, param)
    print(f"  Fixed points found: {len(fixed)}")
    for fp in fixed:
        print(f"    {fp.round(4)}")
    
    # Trajectory from |+>
    traj = bloch_trajectory(channel, param, 20)
    print(f"  Trajectory from |+> (first 5 steps):")
    for i in range(min(5, len(traj))):
        print(f"    Step {i}: {traj[i].round(4)}")
